name: AgentOps Retrieval Brokerage Citations Canary
description: Offline public canary for CASS versus repo-local retrieval brokerage, search/lookup/inject citation accounting, context relevance scoring, and session-intelligence citation telemetry.

cases.title: Retrieval brokerage, citation, context relevance, and telemetry tests pass
cases.objective: Protect behavior that decides between CASS and repo-local search, records adopted retrieval citations, weights relevance with CASS and citation telemetry, and dedupes session-intelligence citation signals.

expectations.value: ok  	github.com/boshu2/agentops/cli/cmd/ao

cases.title: Retrieval brokerage CLI surfaces expose help
cases.objective: Ensure operators can discover search, lookup, inject, citation, and benchmark retrieval surfaces from a built ao binary.

inputs.shell: tmp=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf "$tmp"' EXIT; (cd cli && env -u AGENTOPS_RPI_RUNTIME go build -o "$tmp/ao" ./cmd/ao); "$tmp/ao" search --help >/dev/null; "$tmp/ao" lookup --help >/dev/null; "$tmp/ao" inject --help >/dev/null; "$tmp/ao" retrieval-bench --help >/dev/null

cases.title: Generated CLI docs expose brokerage and citation flags
cases.objective: Keep generated CLI docs aligned with retrieval brokerage defaults and citation-recording controls.

expectations.value: ### `ao search`
Search workspace session history and repo-local AgentOps knowledge.
--cass             Require upstream cass session-history search
--local            Force repo-local AgentOps search only
--cite string      Optional citation type to record for matching repo-local artifacts: retrieved, reference, applied
### `ao lookup`
--cite string      Citation type to record for returned artifacts: retrieved, reference, applied (default "retrieved")
--no-cite          Skip citation recording
### `ao inject`
--no-cite               Disable citation recording
--apply-decay           Apply confidence decay before ranking
--session string        Session ID for citation tracking (auto-generated if empty)

cases.title: Source contracts preserve retrieval brokerage and citation behavior
cases.objective: Lock source-level guarantees for CASS/local search selection, citation recording, context scoring, and citation telemetry normalization.

expectations.value: By default, ao search brokers across two backends:
upstream cass search --workspace <cwd> for session history when cass is available
Use --cass to require upstream cass only.
--cass and --local are mutually exclusive
recordSearchCitations
isRetrievableArtifactPath
searchAuto
searchUpstreamCASS
searchRepoLocalKnowledge
Search query for relevance matching
Skip citation recording
recordLookupCitations
NOTICE: ao inject is deprecated (removal target: v3.0.0). Use 'ao lookup' for learnings or see .agents/AGENTS.md for navigation.
CASS integration adds maturity weighting and confidence decay.
gatherKnowledge collects all knowledge sources and records citations.
recordPatternCitations records citation events for retrieved patterns.
defaultSessionSearchFn returns searchUpstreamCASS if fn is nil.
runtimeCassHitScores(cwd, query, 8, nil)
cassHitWeight
usageSignalForArtifact
type citationAggregate struct
DedupedEvents
normalizeCitationEventForRuntime
annotateCitationMatch
normalizeCitationMatchConfidence

cases.title: Retrieval docs and skills preserve brokerage and citation guidance
cases.objective: Ensure architecture docs and shared/Codex skill contracts describe the retrieval brokerage, assisted citation path, and feedback loop expectations.

expectations.value: Brokers to upstream `cass` for session history and keeps fixed repo-local fallback helpers.
`ao lookup` remains the primary automatic citation path for curated knowledge.
`ao codex start` records `retrieved` citations for the startup artifacts it surfaces.
`ao search --cite retrieved|reference|applied` provides an assisted path when a search result is actually adopted.
Search citation is assisted rather than inferred from every result automatically; the user or skill must choose `--cite` when adoption is known.
`ao search` and `ao lookup` pull prior repo knowledge and nearby precedents.
`lookup`, `search`, notebooks, handoffs, findings, and phase manifests assemble context together
Any runtime surface that injects context must be able to explain:
Use `ao lookup --query "topic"` for on-demand learnings retrieval
After presenting injected knowledge, record which files were injected for the feedback loop:
Citation tracking enables the feedback loop
this hook emits only a pointer to on-demand retrieval commands (`ao search`, `ao lookup`).
Treat `$inject` as passive context loading
Use `$inject` or `ao inject` for on-demand retrieval
After presenting injected knowledge, record which files were injected for the feedback loop:
Citation tracking enables the feedback loop
